title: Kireji Framework Config
description: Schema for the kireji.json configuration file.

includeColor.description: Determines which color mode features will be included in the ecosystem.

oneOf.description: The color component will never be included.
Dark mode will never be included.
Light mode will never be included.
Dark mode will only be included in local builds.
Light mode will only be included in local builds.
The full color component will always be included.

includeEra.description: Determines which era mode features will be included in the ecosystem.

oneOf.description: The era component will never be included.
Modern mode will never be included.
Vintage mode will never be included.
Modern mode will only be included in local builds.
Vintage mode will only be included in local builds.
The full era component will always be included.

includeMenuApps.description: Determines whether a list of apps should be included in the menu.

oneOf.description: Apps will never appear in the menu.
Apps will only appear in the menu in local builds.
Apps will always appear in the menu.

includeUpdates.description: Determines whether or not the version updating features should be included in the menu.

oneOf.description: Update features will never appear in the menu.
Update features will only appear in the menu in local builds.
Update features will always appear in the menu.

includeKirejiApp.description: Determines whether or not the Kireji IDE should be included in the ecosystem.

oneOf.description: kireji.app will never be included.
kireji.app will only be included in local builds.
kireji.app will always be included (used only for Demo App Ecosystem).

includeDesktop.description: Determines whether or not the ecosystem should include desktop-like features.

oneOf.description: The desktop experience will never be included.
Only the menu will be included, and it will always be included.
The full desktop experience will only be included in local builds.
The menu will always be included. The desktop experience will only be included in local builds.
The full desktop experience will always be included (used only for Demo App Ecosystem).

change.description: Represents the severity of the API change for semantic versioning.

oneOf.description: A breaking change which breaks existing hash assignments.
A non-breaking change which adds new hash assignments.
A non-breaking change which does not impact hash assignments at all.

verbosity.description: A number used to control the detail in logs. Only messages with a priority <= this number will be logged.

defaultApplicationHost.description: The host of the desired default app. The server will redirect to this when testing locally.

port.description: The internal port (typically 3000-4000) where the server will be hosted.

mapping.description: Determines whether or not the artifact should be output with embedded source map data.

haltHydration.description: If enabled, halts the hydration of the application completely. Useful for debugging FOUC.

includeWarning.description: If enabled, a warning line appears at the top of all applications regarding alpha status.

resetLocalState.description: If enabled, applications running locally will reset to their landing hash when the service worker is replaced.

hangHydration.description: A string representing how long the main thread should hang to simulate loading. Useful for debugging FOUC.
